LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction01.label: Order
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction01.description: A militant brotherhood dedicated to a strict code of honor and the protection of shrines. Knights in shining armor carry the banner of faith and the sword.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction02.label: Union
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction02.description: An association of cities and craft guilds based on trade agreements and mutual defense. Wealth and diplomacy are their primary weapons.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction03.label: Dynasty
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction03.description: An ancient lineage of rulers whose blood has governed for centuries. Intrigue, dynastic marriages, and an iron will to power define their history.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction04.label: Principality
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction04.description: A mighty principality with a rich history of knightly tournaments, trade, and conquests. Strong castles and loyal vassals guard their borders.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction05.label: Kingdom
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction05.description: A great kingdom with a lavish court and a powerful army. Kings rule with an iron fist, maintaining a balance between the nobility and the church.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction06.label: Barony
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction06.description: A militant barony where the strength of arms is more important than diplomacy. Barons personally lead detachments into battle, protecting their lands from raids.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction07.label: Duchy
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction07.description: A wealthy and cultured duchy, famous for its art, architecture, and subtle diplomacy. Dukes weave complex political networks.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction08.label: County
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction08.description: A merchant county at the crossroads of paths. Caravans, markets, and mercenary crossbowmen ensure prosperity and security.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction09.label: Domain
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction09.description: An extensive domain ruled by a powerful lord. The lands are rich in resources, and the castles are impregnable to enemies.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction10.label: Dominion
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction10.description: An aggressive dominion expanding its borders at the expense of neighbors. Military power and the ambitions of its rulers know no bounds.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction11.label: Vassalage
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction11.description: A network of loyal vassals bound by oaths of fealty to a liege. Honor and duty stand above personal ambitions.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction12.label: Protectorate
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction12.description: A protectorate under the patronage of a powerful state. Peace and stability in exchange for tribute and troops.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction13.label: War Alliance
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction13.description: A union of militant clans hardened by constant wars. Unity in battle is their main principle.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction14.label: Covenant
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction14.description: A mysterious covenant bound by ancient oaths and rituals. Secrecy and devotion to the cause are above all else.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction15.label: Confederation
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction15.description: A free confederation of cities governed by a council of elders. Each city maintains its autonomy.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction16.label: Despotism
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction16.description: A brutal despotism where the will of a single ruler is law for all. Rebellions are suppressed mercilessly and quickly.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction17.label: Abbey
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction17.description: A powerful abbey whose monks combine prayer with the art of war. Knowledge and faith are their strength.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction18.label: Empire
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction18.description: A multi-ethnic empire uniting peoples under the rule of an emperor. Grandeur and power have no limits.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction19.label: Conclave
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction19.description: A secret conclave of mages and scholars ruling from the shadows. Knowledge and magic are their primary tools of power.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction20.label: Guild
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction20.description: A powerful guild of craftsmen and merchants controlling the economy of an entire region. Money rules the world.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction21.label: Mighty Order
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction21.description: A close-knit brotherhood united by a common charter and faith. For them, iron discipline and the protection of a common cause are more important than ancestral lands and titles.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction22.label: Barony
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction22.description: A small but proud domain where every hill is crowned with a tower. The barons here constantly dispute borders, but unite in the face of an external enemy.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction23.label: Margraviate
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction23.description: A military district on the frontier of civilization. Their lives are endless watches and repelling raids, which has made the locals stern and distrustful.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction24.label: Free Brotherhood
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction24.description: A group of mercenaries or exiles living by their own laws. They do not bow to kings, recognizing only the authority of the best fighter and the clink of coin.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction25.label: Diocese
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction25.description: Lands under the direct administration of the church. Here, the word of a cleric weighs more than the steel of a knight, and life is subject to a strict canon.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction26.label: Sejm
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction26.description: A noble assembly where every full member has the right to vote. Constant disputes and vetoes often paralyze power, but make it unique.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction27.label: Appanage
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction27.description: A portion of a once-great kingdom, now living separately. They preserve ancient rites and the hope of one day restoring former greatness.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction28.label: Stronghold
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction28.description: A fortress-city built around an impregnable rock. The inhabitants consider themselves the shield of the world, barring the way to darkness.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction29.label: Nomad Camp
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction29.description: Steppe warriors recognizing only the power of the sky and the horse. Their castles are yurts, and borders are defined by the flight of an arrow.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction30.label: Dominion
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction30.description: Conquered lands held only by fear of a foreign lord. Here, discontent always simmers, suppressed by a hand of steel.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction31.label: Hansa
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction31.description: A union of coastal trading cities. For them, profit is more important than titles, and a fleet is more reliable than any stone wall.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction32.label: Allotment
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction32.description: Land granted to a loyal servant for special merit. Usually, it is a prosperous agrarian community faithful to its benefactor.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction33.label: Covenant
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction33.description: A secret alliance of mystics and scholars hiding in remote mountains. Those around them look upon them with superstitious fear and reverence.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction34.label: Onslaught
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction34.description: An order of fanatical warriors whose purpose for existence is expansion. They do not build cities; they capture them.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction35.label: Votchina
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction35.description: The hereditary estate of an old boyar family. Here they honor the household code, hate innovations, and are faithful to the memory of their grandfathers.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction36.label: Parish
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction36.description: A rural community gathered around an old church. Simple folk seeking solace in labor and faith amidst a harsh world.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction37.label: Citadel
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction37.description: A concentration of the best masters of siege and defense. Their architecture is an ode to stone and medieval engineering thought.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction38.label: Fief
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction38.description: A feudal holding obligated to provide knights at the liege's first call. A classic structure of loyalty and vassalage.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction39.label: Curia
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction39.description: A council of high spiritual and secular nobility, making decisions of truly imperial scale. Laws and curses are born here.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction40.label: Guard
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction40.description: An elite unit that has become an independent political force. Former bodyguards of kings, they now decide for themselves who shall wear the crown.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction41.label: Voivodeship
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction41.description: An administrative unit headed by an experienced military commander. Order is maintained by patrols and heavy fines.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction42.label: Compound
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction42.description: A fortified manor serving as a trade and diplomatic mission. A small island of one culture within another.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction43.label: Veche
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction43.description: A city assembly where decisions are made by the ringing of a bell and the shouts of the crowd. Democracy often turning into a riot.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction44.label: Druzhina
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction44.description: Loyal companions of the prince, bound to him not only by oath but by a common cup. This is not just an army; it is one big family of warriors.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction45.label: Manor
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction45.description: Extensive estates living off peasant labor. A symbol of stability, prosperity, and the slow flow of life.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction46.label: Legion
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction46.description: An attempt to revive ancient discipline in the age of chivalry. Their orderly ranks and tactics often catch unorganized feudal lords by surprise.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction47.label: Appanage
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction47.description: A small principality that has separated from a larger one. It takes pride in its independence despite its modest size.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction48.label: Abbey
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction48.description: A monastery that has become a center of enlightenment and economy. Here they preserve ancient manuscripts and make the best wine in the area.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction49.label: Headquarters
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction49.description: The location of the supreme command of nomadic or mercenary armies. The center for making strategic decisions in field conditions.
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction50.label: Power
LanguageData.MF_MedievalFaction50.description: A large state entity claiming the role of hegemon. Their ambitions are great, and their resources allow them to wage wars on several fronts.
